guide.summary: Your Forehand reaches 48% overall similarity to the pro, with 6 areas worth addressing. The biggest is your Hip (68° off). Power flows from the legs up through trunk rotation into the racket arm.
strengths: Your elbow closely matches the pro (within 1°).
Your shoulder line tilt closely matches the pro (within 4°).

keyIssues.title: Hip: too little vs the pro
keyIssues.observation: Your hips are more extended/upright than the pro's (you 30.75° vs pro 98.88°, about 68° less) — a major gap.
keyIssues.cause: standing too tall means the legs never load, so the stroke becomes all arm.
keyIssues.fix: Prioritize this: sit into a deeper hip load during the unit turn, then drive up and through the ball.

keyIssues.title: Ankle: too little vs the pro
keyIssues.observation: Your ankle is stiffer/less flexed than the pro's (you 103.79° vs pro 159.39°, about 56° less) — a major gap.
keyIssues.cause: limited ankle range reduces ground force and a stable base at contact.
keyIssues.fix: Prioritize this: allow the shin to travel forward and lock the ankle firmly at contact.

keyIssues.title: Shoulder: too much vs the pro
keyIssues.observation: Your arm is carried more abducted (further from the torso) (you 115.12° vs pro 74.35°, about 41° more) — a major gap.
keyIssues.cause: carrying the arm too far from the body disconnects it from the trunk and strains the shoulder.
keyIssues.fix: Prioritize this: keep the elbow closer with a more compact take-back so body rotation carries the racket.

keyIssues.title: Knee: too little vs the pro
keyIssues.observation: Your knee is straighter than the pro's (you 41.38° vs pro 78.63°, about 37° less) — a major gap.
keyIssues.cause: stiff, straight knees skip the leg drive that powers a modern groundstroke.
keyIssues.fix: Prioritize this: add a clear knee bend in the load, then push up off the court through contact.

drills: Loaded open-stance drives — coil onto the back hip and explode up into contact
Compact take-back drill — keep the racket inside the body line on the backswing
Split-step into a loaded knee bend, then an explosive shadow swing
cues: load the outside hip, then drive up
elbow in, let the body turn it

workouts.title: Mobility & activation
workouts.focus: Free up and prime the joints flagged in your Forehand.

warmup.name: 90/90 hip switches
warmup.description: Rotate between hip positions to free the joint.

warmup.name: Knee-to-wall ankle rocks
warmup.description: Drive the knee over the toes to free the ankle.

warmup.name: Band shoulder dislocates
warmup.description: Pass a band overhead and back to open the shoulders.

main.name: Romanian deadlift
main.description: Train the hip hinge and posterior chain. Extra set: this is your largest measured gap (68°).

cooldown.name: Couch stretch
cooldown.description: Open the hip flexors of each leg.

workouts.notes: Do this on training days before skill work.

workouts.title: Corrective strength
workouts.focus: Build strength where your mechanics differ most from the pro: hip (68°), ankle (56°), shoulder (41°).

warmup.name: Dynamic full-body warm-up
warmup.description: Light cardio plus the mobility drills above.

main.name: Romanian deadlift
main.description: Train the hip hinge and posterior chain. Extra set: this is your largest measured gap (68°).

main.name: Single-leg calf raise
main.description: Strengthen the calf and stabilize the ankle. Extra set: this is your largest measured gap (56°).

main.name: Landmine press
main.description: Build stable overhead pressing strength. Extra set: this is your largest measured gap (41°).

cooldown.name: Couch stretch
cooldown.description: Open the hip flexors of each leg.

workouts.notes: 2–3× per week with a day of recovery between sessions. Push the main lifts, but end a set the moment form degrades.

workouts.title: Skill & sequencing
workouts.focus: Transfer the corrections into your tennis forehand.

warmup.name: Shadow reps
warmup.description: Slow-motion Forehand reps focusing on the cues.
cues: load the outside hip, then drive up
elbow in, let the body turn it

main.name: Loaded open-stance drives — coil onto the back hip and explode up into contact
main.description: Rehearse the correction for your hip — you're closing a measured ~68° gap to the pro.
cues: load the outside hip, then drive up

main.name: Banded ankle dorsiflexion mobilizations
main.description: Rehearse the correction for your ankle — you're closing a measured ~56° gap to the pro.

main.name: Compact take-back drill — keep the racket inside the body line on the backswing
main.description: Rehearse the correction for your shoulder — you're closing a measured ~41° gap to the pro.
cues: elbow in, let the body turn it

workouts.notes: Rehearse the full motion slowly, then build speed.
